$comment: Canonical source-of-truth for the DAILY-BRIEFING-NARRATE sprk_analysisplaybook row. R4 task 010 (PR 2) authors; task 011 deploys via dataverse-deploy / Deploy-Playbook.ps1. This playbook is the AiAnalysis grounded-narration playbook the new /narrate wrapper dispatches to via IConsumerRoutingService (Path A.5 — see projects/spaarke-daily-update-service-r4/notes/decisions/030-dispatch-path.md). NOT a Notification playbook (sprk_playbooktype = 0 AiAnalysis, NOT 2 Notification).

_dataverseRow._comment: Maps this document onto the sprk_analysisplaybook Dataverse row. Fields below populate the row at deploy time. The 'sprk_configjson' nested object below is serialized as JSON text into the sprk_configjson Memo column. The 'nodes' + 'edges' arrays below are CONSUMED BY Deploy-Playbook.ps1 to create the associated sprk_analysisnode rows + sprk_playbooknodeconnection rows. Lookup key for upsert is sprk_playbookcode.

fields.sprk_description: Grounded LLM narration of the user's daily notification briefing. Composes a TL;DR + per-channel narrative bullets from a structured DailyBriefingNarrateRequest payload, then scrubs LLM output via EntityNameValidator (ActionType 141) to defeat firm/case-name hallucination. Dispatched by /api/ai/daily-briefing/narrate via IConsumerRoutingService (R4 FR-12 Path A.5).

playbook.name: Daily Briefing Narrate
playbook.description: Grounded LLM narration of the user's daily notification briefing. Composes a TL;DR + per-channel narrative bullets from a structured DailyBriefingNarrateRequest payload, then scrubs LLM output via EntityNameValidator (ActionType 141) to defeat firm/case-name hallucination.

outputBinding._comment: Maps the playbook's final node outputs to the DailyBriefingNarrateResponse contract at src/server/api/Sprk.Bff.Api/Api/Ai/DailyBriefingEndpoints.cs lines 859-901. The new payload-execution method on AnalysisOrchestrationService (task 031) reads these binding hints to project the node graph's terminal output into the response DTO.

composeStrategy._comment: ADR-037 multinode output composition — the parallel GenerateTldr ‖ GenerateChannelNarratives branches fan out from LoadKnowledge then merge at ValidateEntityNames. Per ADR-037 §When-composite, this playbook does NOT use DeliverComposite (ActionType 42 / NodeType 100_000_004) because the response shape is a fixed-arity JSON DTO consumed by the widget — not section-keyed SSE streamed to a widget. Compose semantics: GenerateChannelNarratives is fan-out per channel (one Skill invocation per element of input.channels); outputs are aggregated into an array keyed by the input channel name (preserving input order). GenerateTldr is a single Skill invocation. ValidateEntityNames receives a concatenated candidateText assembled deterministically (tldrResult.summary + tldrResult.keyTakeaways.join + tldrResult.topAction + channelNarrationResults[*].narrative.join) and the merged allow-list. The scrubber's output (scrubbedText) is NOT used to rewrite the response — the response carries the pre-scrub structured fields PLUS scrubbedText metadata for client-side reconciliation. This preserves the structured shape the widget already consumes (TldrResult{summary, keyTakeaways, topAction, ...} + ChannelNarrationResult[]) while ensuring the scrubber's deterministic in-process verification ran (hallucination_detected warnings logged per FR-14).

parameters._comment: Runtime-overridable parameters surfaced to the dispatcher (BFF /narrate wrapper). None required for v1 — payload carries all per-run state.

componentJustification._comment: Required by CLAUDE.md §11 (Component Justification — Default to Reuse) for every new component. Documented here in the canonical playbook JSON so it travels with the artifact.
componentJustification.existing: No existing playbook performs grounded narration of an arbitrary structured-notification payload. The 7 notification-* playbooks (PB-016 through PB-022) PRODUCE notifications via CreateNotification node; they do not CONSUME a payload for narration. The chat-summarize playbooks consume document content, not structured notification arrays.
componentJustification.extension: No. Existing playbooks are notification producers (sprk_playbooktype = 2); this is an AiAnalysis playbook (sprk_playbooktype = 0) consumed by the daily-briefing widget via /narrate. They serve orthogonal consumer-types and cannot share a node graph.
componentJustification.costOfDoingNothing: Spec FR-12 (line 159) is binding: the /narrate endpoint must dispatch to JPS, not run hardcoded prompt strings. Without this playbook the new wrapper has no target to dispatch to — the entire R4 W2 Consumer workstream is blocked (PR 4 tasks 030-036 all depend on this playbook existing in spaarkedev1). The R3 UAT-observed firm/case-name hallucination defect (FR-13 / FR-14 evidence) cannot be remediated end-to-end without this playbook composing the grounded prompt with the scrubber Tool.

configJson.description: Ingest the DailyBriefingNarrateRequest payload from the dispatching wrapper. Shape: { categories: [{category, count, items[]}], priorityItems: [{regardingName, regardingEntityType, viaMatter?, source?, ...}], channels: [{channel, items[]}], totalNotificationCount, // R4-enrichment-fields from FR-6: items[].viaMatter, items[].regardingName, items[].source }. The payload becomes the 'start' scope variable; downstream nodes reference fields via {{start.channels}} / {{start.priorityItems}} / etc.

inputContract.categories: Array<{ category: string, count: number, items: NotificationItem[] }>

configJson._comment: PLACEHOLDER — R5 will bind this to an AI Search 'matter context' knowledge source per spec line 58 ('AI Search matter context knowledge node deferred to R5'). For R4 this node is a no-op pass-through that exposes the input.channels array as channelRegistry.channels for the downstream fan-out. Once R5 wires the AI Search binding, this node will additionally retrieve channel display labels + per-channel category metadata from the registry knowledge source.

r5BindingPlan._comment: R5 will populate this with the actual sprk_knowledge code once the channel-registry knowledge source is authored + indexed.

modelSelection._comment: Temperature 0 + grounded prompt per FR-13. Action row carries the model assignment + sprk_temperature = 0; this node does not override.

inputBinding._comment: Action's 'payload' input (single-string maxLength=50000) receives a JSON-serialized projection of the DailyBriefingNarrateRequest. The payload-execution method serializes start.* fields into the placeholder {{briefing.payload}}. The Action's prompt instructs the LLM to use ONLY entity names present in this payload.

configJson.description: Generate TL;DR (summary + keyTakeaways + topAction + categoryCount + priorityItemCount) from the briefing payload. Runs in parallel with GenerateChannelNarratives. Output schema matches TldrResult contract (DailyBriefingEndpoints.cs:880).

iteration._comment: ADR-037 fan-out semantics per sprk_configjson.composeStrategy.fanOut above — one Skill invocation per element of channelRegistry.channels (which forwards from start.channels via the LoadKnowledge pass-through). Per-iteration the 'channel' alias binds the current iteration item ({channel: string, items: NotificationItem[]}) and is serialized into the Action's 'payload' input (placeholder {{briefing.channel}}).

outputBinding._comment: Per-iteration output is appended to the channelNarrationResults array preserving input order. Each element matches the ChannelNarrationResult contract (DailyBriefingEndpoints.cs:906). For R4 the Action emits { channel, narrative[], itemCount, bulletCount }; the response-composition layer (task 031) maps channel→category and narrative[]→bullets[].narrative to bridge the slight shape difference vs DailyBriefingEndpoints.ChannelNarrationResult (category, bullets[]).

configJson.description: Generate per-channel narrative bullets, one Skill invocation per input channel. Runs in parallel with GenerateTldr. Parallel fan-out per ADR-037. Output is an ordered array of per-channel result objects.

configJson._comment: Post-LLM defense-in-depth scrubber per FR-14. Concatenates all narration text (TL;DR + per-channel) into candidateText; builds allowList from input payload entity-name fields (deduplicated, empty-allowed). MUST execute AFTER both GenerateTldr and GenerateChannelNarratives — the merge of the two parallel branches happens here (per ADR-037 §When-composite + sprk_configjson.composeStrategy above).

inputBinding._comment: R7 Wave 11 T113 rewrite: eliminated inline lambda + pipe-shorthand (both NOT valid Handlebars). candidateText uses nested subexpressions; allowList uses flatMap helper (registered in TemplateEngine.cs) — equivalent semantics, fewer lines, parses cleanly. See docs/architecture/SPAARKE-PLAYBOOK-LLM-OUTPUT-PATTERN.md.
inputBinding.allowList: {{distinct (concat (map start.priorityItems 'regardingName') (map start.priorityItems 'viaMatter.name') (map start.priorityItems 'source.owningUser') (flatMap start.categories 'items.regardingName') (flatMap start.categories 'items.viaMatter.name') (flatMap start.categories 'items.source.owningUser') (flatMap start.channels 'items.regardingName') (flatMap start.channels 'items.viaMatter.name') (flatMap start.channels 'items.source.owningUser'))}}

configJson.description: Scrub LLM output. Allow-list = every regardingName + viaMatter.name + source.owningUser appearing in priorityItems, categories[].items, channels[].items (deduplicated; empty-allowed). Removed terms emit 'hallucination_detected' warning per FR-14 / AC-14a. validationResult.scrubbedText carries the post-scrub merged text; validationResult.removedTerms enumerates every removed proper-noun span. Per the playbook's composeStrategy (sprk_configjson above), the response carries pre-scrub structured fields AND the scrubber's metadata — this preserves the widget's structured-shape contract while still emitting hallucination_detected for monitoring.

configJson._comment: Terminal node — binds final outputs to the playbook-execution method's return value. The payload-execution method on AnalysisOrchestrationService (task 031) reads this binding to project node outputs into DailyBriefingNarrateResponse { Tldr, ChannelNarratives, GeneratedAtUtc } (contract: src/server/api/Sprk.Bff.Api/Api/Ai/DailyBriefingEndpoints.cs:859-872).

_validationMetadata._comment: Optional sidecar surfaced for observability / debugging. The widget does NOT consume these fields today; they are emitted to the response payload for App Insights correlation + future client-side reconciliation per FR-14. Field names use the underscore prefix to mark them as non-contract metadata.

metadata.author: spaarke-daily-update-service-r4 task 010
metadata.description: DAILY-BRIEFING-NARRATE playbook — grounded LLM narration of a structured notification briefing payload, with post-LLM EntityNameValidator scrub. Authors the canonical repo source-of-truth JSON; task 011 deploys via dataverse-deploy. Dispatched by /narrate via IConsumerRoutingService (Path A.5 — see notes/decisions/030-dispatch-path.md). Companion design notes at projects/spaarke-daily-update-service-r4/notes/design/010-daily-briefing-narrate-node-graph.md.

specReferences.FR-13: Grounded prompt content lives in Action rows (lines 166-168)
specReferences.FR-14: Entity-name validator wired into the playbook downstream of both narrate Skills (lines 170-172)

actionGuidsDeployed._comment: Action row GUIDs from PR 1 deployment to spaarkedev1 (tasks 005/006/007). Recorded here for cross-reference during task 011 deploy.
